text: What if practicing different skills together could supercharge your learning? This method is called interleaving practice. Unlike blocked practice, where you repeat one skill, interleaving mixes them up. Research shows that interleaving can improve retention and problem-solving. But why? Cognitive studies suggest that switching tasks forces your brain to make connections, enhancing understanding. So, next time you study, try mixing it up! You’ll not only learn better, but you'll remember longer. Want to boost your learning? Start interleaving today! Follow for more science-backed tips.
